input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time. Switch to **`DynamicResource`** so bindings re-evaluate when the merged dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryText}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when you swap the dictionary -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
```

Then swap themes in code-behind:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

## 2. Android restarts the Activity on UI-mode change

By default Android **recreates** the `Activity` when the system dark-mode setting changes. Tell it to handle the change itself by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With this, toggling dark mode in Android settings delivers a configuration-change callback instead of destroying and recreating the activity.

**TL;DR** — replace every `StaticResource` color binding with `DynamicResource`, and add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to your `MainActivity`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in a .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restart on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android settings, the app restarts. This is because Android recreates the Activity by default when configuration changes (like `uiMode`) occur. They need to handle the `uiMode` configuration change in their Android `MainActivity`.


These are straightforward MAUI issues I can address directly.
